Diagnostic / debug printing for the widget and render trees, plus a tiny leveled logger used by the engine.

A DiagnosticsNode is a name-and-value pair with optional children, used by debugDescribe on Element and RenderObject. Render inspector dumps and the test debug_dump.nim helper print these via prettyPrint.

Types

DiagnosticLevel = enum
  dlHidden, dlFine, dlDebug, dlInfo, dlWarning, dlHint, dlSummary, dlError
Severity tag attached to a DiagnosticsNode. Currently informational; reserved for filtering in a future inspector UI. 
DiagnosticsNode = ref object
  name*: string
  value*: string
  children*: seq[DiagnosticsNode]
  level*: DiagnosticLevel
A node in the diagnostic tree. name is the label, value an optional descriptive string. Children form a sub-tree. 
LogLevel = enum
  llTrace, llDebug, llInfo, llWarn, llError, llFatal
Severity for the engine logger. Ordering is from quietest (llTrace) to loudest (llFatal). The runner emits llInfo messages such as "flit desktop runner started 1024x768".

Vars

minLogLevel = llInfo
Minimum level to actually print. Set lower at startup to enable debug/trace output, e.g. minLogLevel = llDebug.

Procs

proc add(parent: DiagnosticsNode; child: DiagnosticsNode) {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Appends child to parent.children. 
proc add(parent: DiagnosticsNode; name, value: string) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Convenience: builds a leaf node from name and value and appends it. 
proc logf(level: LogLevel; msg: string) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Prints msg to stdout with a level tag (e.g. [info ]). Skips the print if level < minLogLevel. Used by flog and the per-level shortcuts. 
proc node(name: string; value: string = ""; level: DiagnosticLevel = dlInfo): DiagnosticsNode {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Builds a DiagnosticsNode with no children. 
proc prettyPrint(n: DiagnosticsNode; indent = 0): string {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Renders n and its subtree as an indented multi-line string. Each level of nesting adds two spaces. Returns an empty string if n is nil.

Templates

template flog(level: LogLevel; args: varargs[string, `$`])
Stringifies args (via $), joins them, and logs at level. 
template flogd(args: varargs[string, `$`])
Convenience: log at llDebug. 
template floge(args: varargs[string, `$`])
Convenience: log at llError. 
template flogi(args: varargs[string, `$`])
Convenience: log at llInfo. 
template flogw(args: varargs[string, `$`])
Convenience: log at llWarn.
